member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.GetWindowType.GW_HWNDFIRST
member.summary:
The retrieved handle identifies the window of the same type that is highest in the Z order.
If the specified window is a topmost window, the handle identifies a topmost window. If the specified window is a top-level window, the handle identifies a top-level window. If the specified window is a child window, the handle identifies a sibling window.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.GetWindowType.GW_HWNDLAST
member.summary:
The retrieved handle identifies the window of the same type that is lowest in the Z order.
If the specified window is a topmost window, the handle identifies a topmost window. If the specified window is a top-level window, the handle identifies a top-level window. If the specified window is a child window, the handle identifies a sibling window.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.GetWindowType.GW_HWNDNEXT
member.summary:
The retrieved handle identifies the window below the specified window in the Z order.
If the specified window is a topmost window, the handle identifies a topmost window. If the specified window is a top-level window, the handle identifies a top-level window. If the specified window is a child window, the handle identifies a sibling window.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.GetWindowType.GW_HWNDPREV
member.summary:
The retrieved handle identifies the window above the specified window in the Z order.
If the specified window is a topmost window, the handle identifies a topmost window. If the specified window is a top-level window, the handle identifies a top-level window. If the specified window is a child window, the handle identifies a sibling window.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.GetWindowType.GW_OWNER
member.summary: The retrieved handle identifies the specified window's owner window, if any.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.GetWindowType.GW_CHILD
member.summary: The retrieved handle identifies the child window at the top of the Z order, if the specified window is a parent window; otherwise, the retrieved handle is NULL. The function examines only child windows of the specified window. It does not examine descendant windows.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.GetWindowType.GW_ENABLEDPOPUP
member.summary: The retrieved handle identifies the enabled popup window owned by the specified window (the search uses the first such window found using GW_HWNDNEXT); otherwise, if there are no enabled popup windows, the retrieved handle is that of the specified window.

member.name: T:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric
member.summary:
Flags used with the Windows API (User32.dll):GetSystemMetrics(SystemMetric smIndex)
This Enum and declaration signature was written by Gabriel T. Sharp ai_productions@verizon.net or osirisgothra@hotmail.com Obtained on pinvoke.net, please contribute your code to support the wiki!

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_ARRANGE
member.summary: The flags that specify how the system arranged minimized windows. For more information, see the Remarks section in this topic.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CLEANBOOT
member.summary: The value that specifies how the system is started: 0 Normal boot 1 Fail-safe boot 2 Fail-safe with network boot A fail-safe boot (also called SafeBoot, Safe Mode, or Clean Boot) bypasses the user startup files.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CMONITORS
member.summary: The number of display monitors on a desktop. For more information, see the Remarks section in this topic.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CMOUSEBUTTONS
member.summary: The number of buttons on a mouse, or zero if no mouse is installed.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXBORDER
member.summary: The width of a window border, in pixels. This is equivalent to the SM_CXEDGE value for windows with the 3-D look.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXCURSOR
member.summary: The width of a cursor, in pixels. The system cannot create cursors of other sizes.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXDLGFRAME
member.summary: This value is the same as SM_CXFIXEDFRAME.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXDOUBLECLK
member.summary: The width of the rectangle around the location of a first click in a double-click sequence, in pixels. , The second click must occur within the rectangle that is defined by SM_CXDOUBLECLK and SM_CYDOUBLECLK for the system to consider the two clicks a double-click. The two clicks must also occur within a specified time. To set the width of the double-click rectangle, call SystemParametersInfo with SPI_SETDOUBLECLKWIDTH.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXDRAG
member.summary: The number of pixels on either side of a mouse-down point that the mouse pointer can move before a drag operation begins. This allows the user to click and release the mouse button easily without unintentionally starting a drag operation. If this value is negative, it is subtracted from the left of the mouse-down point and added to the right of it.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXEDGE
member.summary: The width of a 3-D border, in pixels. This metric is the 3-D counterpart of SM_CXBORDER.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXFIXEDFRAME
member.summary: The thickness of the frame around the perimeter of a window that has a caption but is not sizable, in pixels. SM_CXFIXEDFRAME is the height of the horizontal border, and SM_CYFIXEDFRAME is the width of the vertical border. This value is the same as SM_CXDLGFRAME.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXFOCUSBORDER
member.summary: The width of the left and right edges of the focus rectangle that the DrawFocusRectdraws. This value is in pixels. Windows 2000: This value is not supported.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXFRAME
member.summary: This value is the same as SM_CXSIZEFRAME.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXFULLSCREEN
member.summary: The width of the client area for a full-screen window on the primary display monitor, in pixels. To get the coordinates of the portion of the screen that is not obscured by the system taskbar or by application desktop toolbars, call the SystemParametersInfofunction with the SPI_GETWORKAREA value.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXHSCROLL
member.summary: The width of the arrow bitmap on a horizontal scroll bar, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXHTHUMB
member.summary: The width of the thumb box in a horizontal scroll bar, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXICON
member.summary: The default width of an icon, in pixels. The LoadIcon function can load only icons with the dimensions that SM_CXICON and SM_CYICON specifies.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXICONSPACING
member.summary: The width of a grid cell for items in large icon view, in pixels. Each item fits into a rectangle of size SM_CXICONSPACING by SM_CYICONSPACING when arranged. This value is always greater than or equal to SM_CXICON.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXMAXIMIZED
member.summary: The default width, in pixels, of a maximized top-level window on the primary display monitor.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXMAXTRACK
member.summary: The default maximum width of a window that has a caption and sizing borders, in pixels. This metric refers to the entire desktop. The user cannot drag the window frame to a size larger than these dimensions. A window can override this value by processing the WM_GETMINMAXINFO message.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXMENUCHECK
member.summary: The width of the default menu check-mark bitmap, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXMENUSIZE
member.summary: The width of menu bar buttons, such as the child window close button that is used in the multiple document interface, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXMIN
member.summary: The minimum width of a window, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXMINIMIZED
member.summary: The width of a minimized window, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXMINSPACING
member.summary: The width of a grid cell for a minimized window, in pixels. Each minimized window fits into a rectangle this size when arranged. This value is always greater than or equal to SM_CXMINIMIZED.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXMINTRACK
member.summary: The minimum tracking width of a window, in pixels. The user cannot drag the window frame to a size smaller than these dimensions. A window can override this value by processing the WM_GETMINMAXINFO message.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXPADDEDBORDER
member.summary: The amount of border padding for captioned windows, in pixels. Windows XP/2000: This value is not supported.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXSCREEN
member.summary: The width of the screen of the primary display monitor, in pixels. This is the same value obtained by calling GetDeviceCaps as follows: GetDeviceCaps( hdcPrimaryMonitor, HORZRES).

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXSIZE
member.summary: The width of a button in a window caption or title bar, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXSIZEFRAME
member.summary: The thickness of the sizing border around the perimeter of a window that can be resized, in pixels. SM_CXSIZEFRAME is the width of the horizontal border, and SM_CYSIZEFRAME is the height of the vertical border. This value is the same as SM_CXFRAME.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXSMICON
member.summary: The recommended width of a small icon, in pixels. Small icons typically appear in window captions and in small icon view.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXSMSIZE
member.summary: The width of small caption buttons, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXVIRTUALSCREEN
member.summary: The width of the virtual screen, in pixels. The virtual screen is the bounding rectangle of all display monitors. The SM_XVIRTUALSCREEN metric is the coordinates for the left side of the virtual screen.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CXVSCROLL
member.summary: The width of a vertical scroll bar, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYBORDER
member.summary: The height of a window border, in pixels. This is equivalent to the SM_CYEDGE value for windows with the 3-D look.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYCAPTION
member.summary: The height of a caption area, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYCURSOR
member.summary: The height of a cursor, in pixels. The system cannot create cursors of other sizes.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYDLGFRAME
member.summary: This value is the same as SM_CYFIXEDFRAME.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYDOUBLECLK
member.summary: The height of the rectangle around the location of a first click in a double-click sequence, in pixels. The second click must occur within the rectangle defined by SM_CXDOUBLECLK and SM_CYDOUBLECLK for the system to consider the two clicks a double-click. The two clicks must also occur within a specified time. To set the height of the double-click rectangle, call SystemParametersInfo with SPI_SETDOUBLECLKHEIGHT.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYDRAG
member.summary: The number of pixels above and below a mouse-down point that the mouse pointer can move before a drag operation begins. This allows the user to click and release the mouse button easily without unintentionally starting a drag operation. If this value is negative, it is subtracted from above the mouse-down point and added below it.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYEDGE
member.summary: The height of a 3-D border, in pixels. This is the 3-D counterpart of SM_CYBORDER.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYFIXEDFRAME
member.summary: The thickness of the frame around the perimeter of a window that has a caption but is not sizable, in pixels. SM_CXFIXEDFRAME is the height of the horizontal border, and SM_CYFIXEDFRAME is the width of the vertical border. This value is the same as SM_CYDLGFRAME.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYFOCUSBORDER
member.summary: The height of the top and bottom edges of the focus rectangle drawn byDrawFocusRect. This value is in pixels. Windows 2000: This value is not supported.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYFRAME
member.summary: This value is the same as SM_CYSIZEFRAME.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYFULLSCREEN
member.summary: The height of the client area for a full-screen window on the primary display monitor, in pixels. To get the coordinates of the portion of the screen not obscured by the system taskbar or by application desktop toolbars, call the SystemParametersInfo function with the SPI_GETWORKAREA value.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYHSCROLL
member.summary: The height of a horizontal scroll bar, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYICON
member.summary: The default height of an icon, in pixels. The LoadIcon function can load only icons with the dimensions SM_CXICON and SM_CYICON.

member.summary: For double byte character set versions of the system, this is the height of the Kanji window at the bottom of the screen, in pixels.

member.summary: The minimum tracking height of a window, in pixels. The user cannot drag the window frame to a size smaller than these dimensions. A window can override this value by processing the WM_GETMINMAXINFO message.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYSCREEN
member.summary: The height of the screen of the primary display monitor, in pixels. This is the same value obtained by calling GetDeviceCaps as follows: GetDeviceCaps( hdcPrimaryMonitor, VERTRES).

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYSIZE
member.summary: The height of a button in a window caption or title bar, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYSIZEFRAME
member.summary: The thickness of the sizing border around the perimeter of a window that can be resized, in pixels. SM_CXSIZEFRAME is the width of the horizontal border, and SM_CYSIZEFRAME is the height of the vertical border. This value is the same as SM_CYFRAME.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYSMCAPTION
member.summary: The height of a small caption, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYSMICON
member.summary: The recommended height of a small icon, in pixels. Small icons typically appear in window captions and in small icon view.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYSMSIZE
member.summary: The height of small caption buttons, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYVIRTUALSCREEN
member.summary: The height of the virtual screen, in pixels. The virtual screen is the bounding rectangle of all display monitors. The SM_YVIRTUALSCREEN metric is the coordinates for the top of the virtual screen.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYVSCROLL
member.summary: The height of the arrow bitmap on a vertical scroll bar, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_CYVTHUMB
member.summary: The height of the thumb box in a vertical scroll bar, in pixels.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_DBCSENABLED
member.summary: Nonzero if User32.dll supports DBCS; otherwise, 0.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_DEBUG
member.summary: Nonzero if the debug version of User.exe is installed; otherwise, 0.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_DIGITIZER
member.summary: Nonzero if the current operating system is Windows 7 or Windows Server 2008 R2 and the Tablet PC Input service is started; otherwise, 0. The return value is a bitmask that specifies the type of digitizer input supported by the device. For more information, see Remarks. Windows Server 2008, Windows Vista, and Windows XP/2000: This value is not supported.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_IMMENABLED
member.summary: Nonzero if Input Method Manager/Input Method Editor features are enabled; otherwise, 0. SM_IMMENABLED indicates whether the system is ready to use a Unicode-based IME on a Unicode application. To ensure that a language-dependent IME works, check SM_DBCSENABLED and the system ANSI code page. Otherwise the ANSI-to-Unicode conversion may not be performed correctly, or some components like fonts or registry settings may not be present.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_MAXIMUMTOUCHES
member.summary: Nonzero if there are digitizers in the system; otherwise, 0. SM_MAXIMUMTOUCHES returns the aggregate maximum of the maximum number of contacts supported by every digitizer in the system. If the system has only single-touch digitizers, the return value is 1. If the system has multi-touch digitizers, the return value is the number of simultaneous contacts the hardware can provide. Windows Server 2008, Windows Vista, and Windows XP/2000: This value is not supported.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_MEDIACENTER
member.summary: Nonzero if the current operating system is the Windows XP, Media Center Edition, 0 if not.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_MENUDROPALIGNMENT
member.summary: Nonzero if drop-down menus are right-aligned with the corresponding menu-bar item; 0 if the menus are left-aligned.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_MIDEASTENABLED
member.summary: Nonzero if the system is enabled for Hebrew and Arabic languages, 0 if not.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_MOUSEPRESENT
member.summary: Nonzero if a mouse is installed; otherwise, 0. This value is rarely zero, because of support for virtual mice and because some systems detect the presence of the port instead of the presence of a mouse.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_MOUSEHORIZONTALWHEELPRESENT
member.summary: Nonzero if a mouse with a horizontal scroll wheel is installed; otherwise 0.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_MOUSEWHEELPRESENT
member.summary: Nonzero if a mouse with a vertical scroll wheel is installed; otherwise 0.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_NETWORK
member.summary: The least significant bit is set if a network is present; otherwise, it is cleared. The other bits are reserved for future use.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_PENWINDOWS
member.summary: Nonzero if the Microsoft Windows for Pen computing extensions are installed; zero otherwise.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_REMOTECONTROL
member.summary: This system metric is used in a Terminal Services environment to determine if the current Terminal Server session is being remotely controlled. Its value is nonzero if the current session is remotely controlled; otherwise, 0. You can use terminal services management tools such as Terminal Services Manager (tsadmin.msc) and shadow.exe to control a remote session. When a session is being remotely controlled, another user can view the contents of that session and potentially interact with it.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_REMOTESESSION
member.summary: This system metric is used in a Terminal Services environment. If the calling process is associated with a Terminal Services client session, the return value is nonzero. If the calling process is associated with the Terminal Services console session, the return value is 0. Windows Server 2003 and Windows XP: The console session is not necessarily the physical console. For more information, seeWTSGetActiveConsoleSessionId.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_SAMEDISPLAYFORMAT
member.summary: Nonzero if all the display monitors have the same color format, otherwise, 0. Two displays can have the same bit depth, but different color formats. For example, the red, green, and blue pixels can be encoded with different numbers of bits, or those bits can be located in different places in a pixel color value.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_SECURE
member.summary: This system metric should be ignored; it always returns 0.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_SERVERR2
member.summary: The build number if the system is Windows Server 2003 R2; otherwise, 0.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_SHOWSOUNDS
member.summary: Nonzero if the user requires an application to present information visually in situations where it would otherwise present the information only in audible form; otherwise, 0.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_SHUTTINGDOWN
member.summary: Nonzero if the current session is shutting down; otherwise, 0. Windows 2000: This value is not supported.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_SLOWMACHINE
member.summary: Nonzero if the computer has a low-end (slow) processor; otherwise, 0.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_STARTER
member.summary: Nonzero if the current operating system is Windows 7 Starter Edition, Windows Vista Starter, or Windows XP Starter Edition; otherwise, 0.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_SWAPBUTTON
member.summary: Nonzero if the meanings of the left and right mouse buttons are swapped; otherwise, 0.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_TABLETPC
member.summary: Nonzero if the current operating system is the Windows XP Tablet PC edition or if the current operating system is Windows Vista or Windows 7 and the Tablet PC Input service is started; otherwise, 0. The SM_DIGITIZER setting indicates the type of digitizer input supported by a device running Windows 7 or Windows Server 2008 R2. For more information, see Remarks.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_XVIRTUALSCREEN
member.summary: The coordinates for the left side of the virtual screen. The virtual screen is the bounding rectangle of all display monitors. The SM_CXVIRTUALSCREEN metric is the width of the virtual screen.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.SystemMetric.SM_YVIRTUALSCREEN
member.summary: The coordinates for the top of the virtual screen. The virtual screen is the bounding rectangle of all display monitors. The SM_CYVIRTUALSCREEN metric is the height of the virtual screen.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.KeyFlag.KF_EXTENDED
member.summary: Manipulates the extended key flag.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.KeyFlag.KF_DLGMODE
member.summary: Manipulates the dialog mode flag, which indicates whether a dialog box is active.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.KeyFlag.KF_MENUMODE
member.summary: Manipulates the menu mode flag, which indicates whether a menu is active.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.KeyFlag.KF_ALTDOWN
member.summary: Manipulates the ALT key flag, which indicated if the ALT key is pressed.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.KeyFlag.KF_REPEAT
member.summary: Manipulates the repeat count.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.KeyFlag.KF_UP
member.summary: Manipulates the transition state flag.

member.name: M:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.UnregisterClass(System.String,System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Unregisters a window class, freeing the memory required for the class.
member.param: Type: LPCTSTR A null-terminated string or a class atom. If lpClassName is a string, it specifies the window class name. This class name must have been registered by a previous call to the RegisterClass or RegisterClassEx function. System classes, such as dialog box controls, cannot be unregistered. If this parameter is an atom, it must be a class atom created by a previous call to the RegisterClass or RegisterClassEx function. The atom must be in the low-order word of lpClassName; the high-order word must be zero.
A handle to the instance of the module that created the class.
member.returns: Type: BOOL If the function succeeds, the return value is nonzero. If the class could not be found or if a window still exists that was created with the class, the return value is zero. To get extended error information, call GetLastError.

member.name: T:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.MonitorInfoEx
member.summary: The MONITORINFOEX structure contains information about a display monitor. The GetMonitorInfo function stores information into a MONITORINFOEX structure or a MONITORINFO structure. The MONITORINFOEX structure is a superset of the MONITORINFO structure. The MONITORINFOEX structure adds a string member to contain a name for the display monitor.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.MonitorInfoEx.Size
member.summary: The size, in bytes, of the structure. Set this member to sizeof(MONITORINFOEX) (72) before calling the GetMonitorInfo function. Doing so lets the function determine the type of structure you are passing to it.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.MonitorInfoEx.Monitor
member.summary: A RECT structure that specifies the display monitor rectangle, expressed in virtual-screen coordinates. Note that if the monitor is not the primary display monitor, some of the rectangle's coordinates may be negative values.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.MonitorInfoEx.WorkArea
member.summary: A RECT structure that specifies the work area rectangle of the display monitor that can be used by applications, expressed in virtual-screen coordinates. Windows uses this rectangle to maximize an application on the monitor. The rest of the area in rcMonitor contains system windows such as the task bar and side bars. Note that if the monitor is not the primary display monitor, some of the rectangle's coordinates may be negative values.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.MonitorInfoEx.Flags
member.summary:
The attributes of the display monitor.
This member can be the following value: 1 : MONITORINFOF_PRIMARY

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.MonitorInfoEx.DeviceName
member.summary: A string that specifies the device name of the monitor being used. Most applications have no use for a display monitor name, and so can save some bytes by using a MONITORINFO structure.

member.name: T:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.RectStruct
member.summary: The RECT structure defines the coordinates of the upper-left and lower-right corners of a rectangle.
member.remarks: By convention, the right and bottom edges of the rectangle are normally considered exclusive. In other words, the pixel whose coordinates are ( right, bottom ) lies immediately outside of the the rectangle. For example, when RECT is passed to the FillRect function, the rectangle is filled up to, but not including, the right column and bottom row of pixels. This structure is identical to the RECTL structure.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.RectStruct.Left
member.summary: The x-coordinate of the upper-left corner of the rectangle.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.RectStruct.Top
member.summary: The y-coordinate of the upper-left corner of the rectangle.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.RectStruct.Right
member.summary: The x-coordinate of the lower-right corner of the rectangle.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.RectStruct.Bottom
member.summary: The y-coordinate of the lower-right corner of the rectangle.

member.name: T:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.TMEFlags
member.summary: The services requested. This member can be a combination of the following values.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.TMEFlags.TME_CANCEL
member.summary: The caller wants to cancel a prior tracking request. The caller should also specify the type of tracking that it wants to cancel. For example, to cancel hover tracking, the caller must pass the TME_CANCEL and TME_HOVER flags.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.TMEFlags.TME_HOVER
member.summary: The caller wants hover notification. Notification is delivered as a WM_MOUSEHOVER message. If the caller requests hover tracking while hover tracking is already active, the hover timer will be reset. This flag is ignored if the mouse pointer is not over the specified window or area.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.TMEFlags.TME_LEAVE
member.summary: The caller wants leave notification. Notification is delivered as a WM_MOUSELEAVE message. If the mouse is not over the specified window or area, a leave notification is generated immediately and no further tracking is performed.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.TMEFlags.TME_NONCLIENT
member.summary: The caller wants hover and leave notification for the nonclient areas. Notification is delivered as WM_NCMOUSEHOVER and WM_NCMOUSELEAVE messages.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.TMEFlags.TME_QUERY
member.summary: The function fills in the structure instead of treating it as a tracking request. The structure is filled such that had that structure been passed to TrackMouseEvent, it would generate the current tracking. The only anomaly is that the hover time-out returned is always the actual time-out and not HOVER_DEFAULT, if HOVER_DEFAULT was specified during the original TrackMouseEvent request.

member.name: M:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.GetForegroundWindow
member.summary:
Retrieves a handle to the foreground window (the window with which the user is currently working). The system assigns a slightly higher priority to the thread that creates the foreground window than it does to other threads.
See https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/ms633505%28v=vs.85%29.aspx for more information.
member.returns:
C++ ( Type: Type: HWND )
The return value is a handle to the foreground window. The foreground window can be NULL in certain circumstances, such as when a window is losing activation.

member.name: M:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.DestroyWindow(System.IntPtr)

summary.para: The DestroyWindow function destroys the specified window. The function sends WM_DESTROY and WM_NCDESTROY messages to the window to deactivate it and remove the keyboard focus from it. The function also destroys the window's menu, flushes the thread message queue, destroys timers, removes clipboard ownership, and breaks the clipboard viewer chain (if the window is at the top of the viewer chain).

If the specified window is a parent or owner window, DestroyWindow automatically destroys the associated child or owned windows when it destroys the parent or owner window. The function first destroys child or owned windows, and then it destroys the parent or owner window.
DestroyWindow also destroys modeless dialog boxes created by the CreateDialog function.

member.param: Handle to the window to be destroyed.
member.returns: If the function succeeds, the return value is nonzero. If the function fails, the return value is zero. To get extended error information, call GetLastError.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_ACCEPTFILES
member.summary: Specifies a window that accepts drag-drop files.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_APPWINDOW
member.summary: Forces a top-level window onto the taskbar when the window is visible.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_CLIENTEDGE
member.summary: Specifies a window that has a border with a sunken edge.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_COMPOSITED
member.summary: Specifies a window that paints all descendants in bottom-to-top painting order using double-buffering. This cannot be used if the window has a class style of either CS_OWNDC or CS_CLASSDC. This style is not supported in Windows 2000.
member.remarks: With WS_EX_COMPOSITED set, all descendants of a window get bottom-to-top painting order using double-buffering. Bottom-to-top painting order allows a descendent window to have translucency (alpha) and transparency (color-key) effects, but only if the descendent window also has the WS_EX_TRANSPARENT bit set. Double-buffering allows the window and its descendents to be painted without flicker.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_CONTEXTHELP
member.summary: Specifies a window that includes a question mark in the title bar. When the user clicks the question mark, the cursor changes to a question mark with a pointer. If the user then clicks a child window, the child receives a WM_HELP message. The child window should pass the message to the parent window procedure, which should call the WinHelp function using the HELP_WM_HELP command. The Help application displays a pop-up window that typically contains help for the child window. WS_EX_CONTEXTHELP cannot be used with the WS_MAXIMIZEBOX or WS_MINIMIZEBOX styles.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_CONTROLPARENT
member.summary: Specifies a window which contains child windows that should take part in dialog box navigation. If this style is specified, the dialog manager recurses into children of this window when performing navigation operations such as handling the TAB key, an arrow key, or a keyboard mnemonic.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_DLGMODALFRAME
member.summary: Specifies a window that has a double border.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_LAYERED
member.summary: Specifies a window that is a layered window. This cannot be used for child windows or if the window has a class style of either CS_OWNDC or CS_CLASSDC.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_LAYOUTRTL
member.summary: Specifies a window with the horizontal origin on the right edge. Increasing horizontal values advance to the left. The shell language must support reading-order alignment for this to take effect.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_LEFT
member.summary: Specifies a window that has generic left-aligned properties. This is the default.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_LEFTSCROLLBAR
member.summary: Specifies a window with the vertical scroll bar (if present) to the left of the client area. The shell language must support reading-order alignment for this to take effect.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_LTRREADING
member.summary: Specifies a window that displays text using left-to-right reading-order properties. This is the default.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_MDICHILD
member.summary: Specifies a multiple-document interface (MDI) child window.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_NOACTIVATE
member.summary: Specifies a top-level window created with this style does not become the foreground window when the user clicks it. The system does not bring this window to the foreground when the user minimizes or closes the foreground window. The window does not appear on the taskbar by default. To force the window to appear on the taskbar, use the WS_EX_APPWINDOW style. To activate the window, use the SetActiveWindow or SetForegroundWindow function.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_NOINHERITLAYOUT
member.summary: Specifies a window which does not pass its window layout to its child windows.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_NOPARENTNOTIFY
member.summary: Specifies that a child window created with this style does not send the WM_PARENTNOTIFY message to its parent window when it is created or destroyed.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_OVERLAPPEDWINDOW
member.summary: Specifies an overlapped window.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_PALETTEWINDOW
member.summary: Specifies a palette window, which is a modeless dialog box that presents an array of commands.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_RIGHT
member.summary: Specifies a window that has generic "right-aligned" properties. This depends on the window class. The shell language must support reading-order alignment for this to take effect. Using the WS_EX_RIGHT style has the same effect as using the SS_RIGHT (static), ES_RIGHT (edit), and BS_RIGHT/BS_RIGHTBUTTON (button) control styles.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_RIGHTSCROLLBAR
member.summary: Specifies a window with the vertical scroll bar (if present) to the right of the client area. This is the default.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_RTLREADING
member.summary: Specifies a window that displays text using right-to-left reading-order properties. The shell language must support reading-order alignment for this to take effect.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_STATICEDGE
member.summary: Specifies a window with a three-dimensional border style intended to be used for items that do not accept user input.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_TOOLWINDOW
member.summary: Specifies a window that is intended to be used as a floating toolbar. A tool window has a title bar that is shorter than a normal title bar, and the window title is drawn using a smaller font. A tool window does not appear in the taskbar or in the dialog that appears when the user presses ALT+TAB. If a tool window has a system menu, its icon is not displayed on the title bar. However, you can display the system menu by right-clicking or by typing ALT+SPACE.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_TOPMOST
member.summary: Specifies a window that should be placed above all non-topmost windows and should stay above them, even when the window is deactivated. To add or remove this style, use the SetWindowPos function.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_TRANSPARENT
member.summary: Specifies a window that should not be painted until siblings beneath the window (that were created by the same thread) have been painted. The window appears transparent because the bits of underlying sibling windows have already been painted. To achieve transparency without these restrictions, use the SetWindowRgn function.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.User32.WindowStylesEx.WS_EX_WINDOWEDGE
member.summary: Specifies a window that has a border with a raised edge.

member.name: M:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.Kernel32.VerSetConditionMask(System.UInt64,System.UInt32,System.Byte)
member.summary: Sets the bits of a 64-bit value to indicate the comparison operator to use for a specified operating system version attribute. This function is used to build the dwlConditionMask parameter of the RtlVerifyVersionInfo(NativeTypes.OSVERSIONINFOEX, uint, ulong) and
member.param: A value to be passed as the dwlConditionMask parameter of the RtlVerifyVersionInfo(NativeTypes.OSVERSIONINFOEX, uint, ulong) and

A mask that indicates the member of NativeTypes.OSVERSIONINFOEX"/> whose comparision operator is being set. This value corresponds to one of the bits specified in the dwTypeMask parameter of RtlVerifyVersionInfo(NativeTypes.OSVERSIONINFOEX, uint, ulong). This parameter can have one of the values from NativeConstants.TypeBitMasks
member.returns: The function returns the condition mask value.
member.remarks: Before the first call to this function, initialize variable to zero. For subsequent calls, pass in the variable used in the previous call.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.PRODUCT_SUITE.VER_SUITE_BACKOFFICE
member.summary: Microsoft BackOffice components are installed.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.PRODUCT_SUITE.VER_SUITE_BLADE
member.summary: Windows Server 2003, Web Edition is installed.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.PRODUCT_SUITE.VER_SUITE_COMPUTE_SERVER
member.summary: Windows Server 2003, Compute Cluster Edition is installed.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.PRODUCT_SUITE.VER_SUITE_DATACENTER
member.summary: Windows Server 2008 Datacenter, Windows Server 2003, Datacenter Edition, or Windows 2000 Datacenter Server is installed.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.PRODUCT_SUITE.VER_SUITE_ENTERPRISE
member.summary: Windows Server 2008 Enterprise, Windows Server 2003, Enterprise Edition, or Windows 2000 Advanced Server is installed.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.PRODUCT_SUITE.VER_SUITE_EMBEDDEDNT
member.summary: Windows XP Embedded is installed.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.PRODUCT_SUITE.VER_SUITE_PERSONAL
member.summary: Windows Vista Home Premium, Windows Vista Home Basic, or Windows XP Home Edition is installed.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.PRODUCT_SUITE.VER_SUITE_SINGLEUSERTS
member.summary: Remote Desktop is supported, but only one interactive session is supported. This value is set unless the system is running in application server mode.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.PRODUCT_SUITE.VER_SUITE_SMALLBUSINESS
member.summary: Microsoft Small Business Server was once installed on the system, but may have been upgraded to another version of Windows.
member.remarks: You should not rely solely on the flag to determine whether Small Business Server is currently installed. Both this flag and the flag are set when this product suite is installed. If you upgrade this installation to Windows Server, Standard Edition, the flag is cleared, but the flag remains set, which, in this case, indicates that Small Business Server was previously installed on this system. If this installation is further upgraded to Windows Server, Enterprise Edition, the flag remains set.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.PRODUCT_SUITE.VER_SUITE_SMALLBUSINESS_RESTRICTED
member.summary: Microsoft Small Business Server is installed with the restrictive client license in force. For more information about this flag bit, see the remarks for flag.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.PRODUCT_SUITE.VER_SUITE_STORAGE_SERVER
member.summary: Windows Storage Server 2003 R2 or Windows Storage Server 2003 is installed.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.PRODUCT_SUITE.VER_SUITE_TERMINAL
member.summary: Terminal Services is installed. This value is always set. If is set but is not set, the operating system is running in application server mode.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.PRODUCT_SUITE.VER_SUITE_WH_SERVER
member.summary: Windows Home Server is installed.

member.name: T:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.OSVERSIONINFOEX
member.summary: The RTL_OSVERSIONINFOEXW structure contains operating system version information.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.OSVERSIONINFOEX.dwOSVersionInfoSize
member.summary: The size, in bytes, of an RTL_OSVERSIONINFOEXW structure. This member must be set before the structure is used with RtlGetVersion.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.OSVERSIONINFOEX.dwMajorVersion
member.summary: The major version number of the operating system. For example, for Windows 2000, the major version number is five.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.OSVERSIONINFOEX.dwMinorVersion
member.summary: The minor version number of the operating system. For example, for Windows 2000, the minor version number is zero.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.OSVERSIONINFOEX.dwBuildNumber
member.summary: The build number of the operating system.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.OSVERSIONINFOEX.dwPlatformId
member.summary: The operating system platform. For Win32 on NT-based operating systems, RtlGetVersion returns the value VER_PLATFORM_WIN32_NT.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.OSVERSIONINFOEX.szCSDVersion
member.summary: The service-pack version string. This member contains a null-terminated string, such as "Service Pack 3", which indicates the latest service pack installed on the system. If no service pack is installed, RtlGetVersion might not initialize this string. Initialize szCSDVersion to zero (empty string) before the call to RtlGetVersion.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.OSVERSIONINFOEX.wServicePackMajor
member.summary: The major version number of the latest service pack installed on the system. For example, for Service Pack 3, the major version number is three. If no service pack has been installed, the value is zero.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.OSVERSIONINFOEX.wServicePackMinor
member.summary: The minor version number of the latest service pack installed on the system. For example, for Service Pack 3, the minor version number is zero.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.OSVERSIONINFOEX.wSuiteMask
member.summary: The product suites available on the system. This member is set to zero or to the bitwise OR of one or more of the values.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.OSVERSIONINFOEX.wProductType
member.summary: The product type. This member contains additional information about the system.

member.name: F:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.OSVERSIONINFOEX.wReserved
member.summary: Reserved for future use.

member.name: M:OverlayDearImGui.Windows.OSVERSIONINFOEX.Create
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct with set to the correct value.

member.remarks: Note that a server that is also a domain controller is reported as , not .
